 4. release cache in hsm restore
 5.rename lcoc to pcc as Andreas suggested.

One defect here:

1) hsm restore need be atomic with pcc detach, one good way might make it in ll_md_blocking_ast(), but that might be also problems if we don't know Layout change
comes from HSM restore.

Any good idea for this? Also it would be nice if you could review this early drafted patch.

Thanks,
Shilong

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

(24 comments)

I think there are two main questions to be answered before we can consider landing this:
- do we want to go directly to mounting whole filesystem images that are accessed directly on the client, or should the "automatic mount of ext4 filesystem image" be a separate (optional) feature of files stored in Lustre (regardless of PCC or remote)
- is PCC duplicating fscache/cachefs/cachefiles too much, and we should look at using those to avoid code duplication and ease upstream acceptance, or is PCC faster and more flexible for our use?

Patch Set 10: Code-Review-1

(83 comments)

This is a partial review. I'd like to think about this more.

The path and permission handling need clarification.

How do the HSM CDT and other clients handle it when HSM restore returns -EBUSY?

What happens when two clients try to PCC the same file?

In the ct_restore() we end up calling search_inode_for_lustre() to detach the file from PCC. But we don't need to detach the inode if it wasn't already cache. So can we use ilookup5() rather than search_inode_for_lustre() for detach?

commit.subject: LU-10092 llite: Add persistent cache on client
commit.message:
LU-10092 llite: Add persistent cache on client

PCC is a new framework which provides a group of local cache
on Lustre client side. No global namespace will be provided
by PCC. Each client uses its own local storage as a cache for
itself. Local file system is used to manage the data on local
caches. Cached I/O is directed to local filesystem while
normal I/O is directed to OSTs.

PCC uses HSM for data synchronization. It uses HSM copytool
to restore file from local caches to Lustre OSTs. Each PCC
has a copytool instance running with unique archive number.
Any remote access from another Lustre client would trigger
the data synchronization. If a client with PCC goes offline,
the cached data becomes inaccessible for other client
temporarilly. And after the PCC client reboots and the copytool
restarts, the data will be accessible again.

ToDo:
1)Make PCC exclusive with HSM.
